title: Lithuanians stand in vast chain of support for Belarus opposition - Reuters Africa
content: Lithuanians stood in a 35,000-strong human chain stretching 34 km (21 miles) from central Vilnius to the Belarus border on Sunday to show support for protesters in Belarus and opposition leader Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya, who has taken refuge in the country.

title: Florida governor removes COVID-19 restrictions on restaurants, bars - Reuters UK
content: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis on Friday lifted all COVID-19 restrictions on businesses, including restaurants and bars, saying the threat of the coronavirus pandemic had eased sufficiently to allow the state to enter Phase 3 of its reopening.

title: UPDATE 1-Detroit officer charged with shooting journalists with rubber pellets - Reuters India
content: UPDATE 1-Detroit officer charged with shooting journalists with rubber pellets  Reuters India

title: U.S. 30-Year Yields Near Top of Range, PGIM's Tipp Says
Supreme Court Won’t Lift Pennsylvania Governor’s Shutdown Order
content: Supreme Court Won’t Lift Pennsylvania Governor’s Shutdown Orderbloomberg.com

title: Frankfurt Book Fair to Take Place This Year Despite Coronavirus
UPDATE 1-KB Financial Group acquires Prudential's S.Korean unit for $1.89 bln
content: KB Financial Group Inc <105560.KS> has signed a share purchase agreement to buy a 100% stake in Prudential Financial Inc's South Korean unit for 2.3 trillion won ($1.89 billion), the group said in a filing on Friday.

title: Czech coalition agrees 5% digital tax aimed at global internet giants- CTK agency
content:
The two Czech ruling coalition
parties agreed on Wednesday to impose a 5% digital tax aimed at
global internet giants, below an earlier plan for a 7% rate,
news agency CTK quoted Labour Minister Jana Malacova as saying
on Wednesday.

title: Oil falls as demand worries counter U.S. stimulus hopes
content: Oil prices fell on Tuesday, paring gains from the previous session, as persistent demand concerns due to the coronavirus pandemic outweighed hopes for a new U.S. stimulus package.

title: England set Windies 200-run victory target in first test
content: England were bowled out for 313 in their second innings at the Ageas Bowl on Sunday, setting the West Indies a victory target of 200 on the final day of the first test.

title: Gender divide: Peru limits men and women to alternate days out to stall virus
content: Amid a strict quarantine in Peru to slow the spread of a coronavirus pandemic, men and women will only be allowed to leave their homes on designed days divided up by gender, the country's President Martin Vizcarra said on Thursday.

title: Venezuela court jails two U.S. ex-soldiers for 20 years after failed incursion - Reuters UK
content: A Venezuelan court sentenced two former U.S. soldiers to 20 years in prison for their role in a failed incursion aimed at ousting President Nicolas Maduro in early May, chief prosecutor Tarek Saab said late on Friday.

title: South Sudan president replaces finance minister, state TV reports - Reuters UK
content: South Sudan President Salva Kiir has removed the country's finance minister Salvatore Garang from office, replacing him with Athian Ding Athian, state television reported on Wednesday.

title: Delta to Tap Jets for $1 Billion in Cash Amid Talks for U.S. Aid
Deutsche Bank, BlackRock CEOs warn against too much optimism in markets
content: The heads of Deutsche Bank and BlackRock on Wednesday warned against too much optimism in financial markets.

title: Wirecard ex-COO Marsalek's entry into Philippines was faked, minister says
content: Immigration records showing Wirecard's former chief operating officer Jan Marsalek arrived in the Philippines on June 23 and departed for China the next day were falsified, Philippines Justice Secretary Menardo Guevarra said on Saturday.

title: Paris police attacked, 158 arrested after PSG defeat - Reuters Africa
content: Paris police were attacked, shops in the French capital were vandalised and 158 people were arrested after Paris Saint Germain's loss to Bayern Munich in the Champions League soccer final, the interior minister and police said on Monday.

title: Bloomberg Markets: China Open Full Show (06/11/2020)
Powell Says Getting Virus Under Control Is Vital for Economy
UPDATE 1-Hit by coronavirus, Fiat Chrysler, Peugeot seek to boost cash before merger consummation
content: With their sales hammered by the coronavirus outbreak, car makers Fiat Chrysler and Peugeot's owner PSA have postponed their shareholder meetings and are looking at ways to boost cash reserves ahead of their planned merger.

title: Tennis: Women's WTA Tour not giving up yet on China events - Reuters
content: The Women's WTA Tour has not given up hopes on its tournaments in China despite the country's sports authority saying it will not hold any international sporting events this year aside from trials for the 2022 Beijing Winter Olympics.

title: Australian regulator urges banks, insurers to defer dividends amid virus outbreak
content: The Australian financial regulator on Tuesday urged banks and insurers to consider deferring dividend payouts or use buffers like dividend reinvestment plans until the impact of the coronavirus pandemic is better known.

title: FOCUS-U.S. auto suppliers scramble to fill factory jobs
content: Millions of U.S. workers have lost their jobs to the pandemic, but in the auto industry, suppliers are scrambling to find enough people to staff production lines, resorting to such approaches as rewards for good attendance and at-work teachers to lure job see…

title: Turkish drill ship heads back to Turkey from off Cyprus - data - Reuters
content: The Turkish drill ship Yavuz has left the area where it was operating southwest of Cyprus and reached the Turkish coast, Refinitiv Eikon shipping data showed on Monday, in a move that may help ease tensions in the eastern Mediterranean.

title: EU's Vestager denies making Lufthansa bailout more difficult
content: EU antitrust chief Margrethe Vestager denied on Friday that she was putting hurdles in the way of Lufthansa's $10 billion government rescue, saying companies getting big capital injections from the state have to offset their competitive advantage.

title: Three Republicans call for layoff language fix before Senate coronavirus bill vote
content: Three Republican U.S. senators on Wednesday said the Senate should not vote on a $2 trillion coronavirus stimulus bill until legislative language that they say would encourage layoffs is fixed or fresh guidance issued from the Labor Department.

title: Werenski and Thompson tied for lead after second round in Minnesota
content: For the second day in a row Richy Werenski and Michael Thompson found themselves knotted atop the leaderboard at the 3M Open with the two Americans poised to settle the score in Sunday's final round in Blaine, Minnesota.

title: Dollar in narrow range as U.S. virus cases grow - Reuters
content: The dollar was hemmed into a narrow range on Friday, supported by safe-haven flows as a resurgence of the coronavirus in the United States discouraged some investors from taking on excessive risk.

title: UK joins U.S., U.N. in supporting Saudi-led ceasefire in Yemen amid virus outbreak
content: British foreign minister Dominic Raab said on Thursday that the United Kingdom supported the Saudi-led ceasefire in Yemen that went into effect on Thursday amid the coronavirus outbreak and has raised hope for an end to the five-year-old war.

title: Insurgency, Virus Threaten 10.6 Million in Northeast Nigeria
content: At least 10.6 million people in Nigeria’s northeast urgently need aid as a combination of Covid-19 restrictions and increasing insurgent attacks put the civilian population in peril, according to the United Nations.
